name: Hendaye and Puerto de Erlaitz
name_original: Hendaye y Puerto de Erlaitz — circular desde Irún
description: This moderate 24.5-mile road cycling route from Irún features the challenging Puerto de Erlaitz climb and panoramic Txingudi Bay views.
region_intro: Hiking around Bajo Bidasoa offers diverse landscapes in the province of Gipuzkoa, Basque Country, Spain. The region is characterized by its unique geographical position along the lower Bidasoa River and the Bay of Txingudi, bordering France and Navarre. It features a mix of coastal paths, mountain ranges like Jaizkibel, and natural parks such as Aiako Harria, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Bajo Bidasoa The most popular hiking route is The coastal path of the Higuer Lighthouse — circular from the lighthouse, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the coastline and the Higuer Lighthouse. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñas de Aia: Erroilbide, Txurrumurru, Irumugarrieta y Muganix — circular por el Parque Natural Aiako Harria, a difficult 4.6 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail explores the mountainous terrain within the Aiako Harria Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Cascada de Aitzondo (SL-Gi 1006) — circular through the Aiako Harria Natural Park, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through the Aiako Harria Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Bajo Bidasoa is defined by coastal paths, mountain trails, and river routes, including sections of the Bidasoa Greenway. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Bajo Bidasoa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,300 reviews. More than 10,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bajo Bidasoa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: Bajo Bidasoa offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 280 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: The region boasts diverse landscapes, including coastal paths along the Bay of Txingudi, mountain trails in areas like Jaizkibel and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1774319/peaks-around-bajo-bidasoa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aiako Harria Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic river routes along the Bidasoa River. You'll encounter a mix of forests, open vistas, and sometimes rocky sections.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: The trails in Bajo Bidasoa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,300 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied scenery, well-maintained paths, and the stunning views, especially from coastal and mountain routes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: Bajo Bidasoa is suitable for hiking year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal walks, while winter can provide unique, quieter experiences, though some higher mountain trails might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1841170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aiako Harria Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1841170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aitzondo waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, one of Gipuzkoa's largest. You can explore this area on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047331276/cascada-de-aitzondo-sl-gi-1006-circular-through-the-aiako-harria-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Aitzondo (SL-Gi 1006) — circular through the Aiako Harria Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider hiking in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1427010310/jaizkibel-mountain-range" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaizkibel mountain range&lt;/a&gt;. Its highest point, S. Enrique (547 meters), offers stunning vistas of the Bay of Hendaye, the Atlantic coast, and surrounding Spanish territory. San Marcial Mountain in Irun also provides excellent views of the city, the Bidasoa River, and Txingudi Bay.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: Yes, Bajo Bidasoa offers several easy and moderate trails suitable for families. The Bidasoa Greenway, which follows an old mining train track, is a great option for a relaxed walk or cycle through picturesque landscapes. Many coastal paths also provide accessible and enjoyable experiences for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Bajo Bidasoa are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323803633/el-valle-de-los-colores-circular-desde-el-parador-de-jaizkibel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Valle de los colores — circular desde el Parador de Jaizkibel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1320467261/faro-de-higuer-circular-desde-guadalupe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro de Higuer — circular desde Guadalupe&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Bajo Bidasoa?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Bajo Bidasoa, especially in natural parks and rural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to check specific park regulations beforehand. Always clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Bajo Bidasoa has 87 easy trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. The Bidasoa Greenway is an excellent choice, offering flat and well-maintained paths. Many sections of the coastal paths are also considered easy and provide beautiful scenery without significant elevation changes.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options, including local buses, connect various towns and villages within Bajo Bidasoa, which can provide access to some trailheads. For specific routes, it's recommended to check local bus schedules and routes in advance to plan your journey effectively.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural routes in the region?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history reflected in its trails. The Bidasoa Greenway follows an old mining train track, offering a glimpse into the area's industrial past. Additionally, the Smugglers' Route of Bidasoa (Kontrabandistak Bidea) is a circular path that traverses mountain trails historically used for smuggling, combining natural beauty with cultural significance.

Q: What are some unique natural attractions to look out for?
A: Beyond the well-known natural parks, you can discover unique geological features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5653656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sandstone Cliff with Fossil Formations&lt;/a&gt;, the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/189260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlatz Beach Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4639053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erantzintxiki Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;. These highlights offer fascinating insights into the region's natural history and beauty.
tips: Located on the border with Iparralde, Irun, it has been the scene of epic battles, which destroyed a large part of its heritage. However, its more than 2,000 years of history have left a unique architectural legacy worth visiting. In addition, beautiful mountains such as Peñas de Aia (837 m.) and Jaizkibel (545 m.), as well as the top of San Marcial, surround this Gipuzkoan municipality.
The view of the sea is great. When it's windy, the route should be viewed as a challenge
A very hard climb of 3.8km with an average of 11.1%. It has very hard ramps but it is a nice climb with a good road. From above you can see to one side the entire Txingudi bay.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Puerto de Erlaitz climb on this route?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated as moderate, the ascent to Erlaitz Pass is considered very challenging. It features 3.8 km with an average gradient of 11.1%, including ramps with constant double-digit gradients. Cyclists often refer to it as a 'very hard climb' and a 'mythical port' due to its steepness and history in professional races like the Clásica San Sebastián.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Hendaye and Puerto de Erlaitz circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. However, be prepared for 'very hard ramps' with significant gradients during the climb to Puerto de Erlaitz, even though the road quality remains good.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the Irún Historic Center, and ride along the Boulevard de la Mer - Hendaye. The most spectacular views are from the summit of Erlaitz Pass, offering panoramic vistas of the entire Txingudi Bay.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region around Irún and Hendaye generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. However, for the best experience, consider cycling during spring or early autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is typically more stable. Always check local weather forecasts before you go, especially for the mountain sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this circular route in Irún?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, Irún, being the starting point, offers various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the historic center or other accessible areas in Irún to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Is the Hendaye and Puerto de Erlaitz route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to the significant elevation gain and the 'very hard ramps' of the Puerto de Erlaitz climb, this route is not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good fitness and experience with challenging road cycling ascents.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the CIMA GP04 Erlaitz (Castillo del Inglés), Oiartzundik Aiako Harrira, Northern Route (Basque Country), and La route des cols Hendaye - Larrau, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling this route, especially within Aiako Harria Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the public areas of Aiako Harria Natural Park through which it passes. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, having a dog accompany you on a road bike, especially on challenging climbs with traffic, is not practical or safe. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this specific cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through urban areas like Irún and Hendaye, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. The Irún Historic Center and Boulevard de la Mer - Hendaye are good places to look for amenities.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the significant climb and potential for varied weather, it's essential to wear appropriate road cycling attire, including a helmet, padded shorts, and layers that can be adjusted. Bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Sun protection is also recommended.
